title: SynGFN: learning across chemical space with generative flow-based molecular discovery
methods: generative flow network (GFlowNet) with trajectory balance loss
hierarchical dual-agent policy network (reaction selection + reactant selection)
multilabel classification pretraining for reactant policy
multifidelity Bayesian optimization (QSAR proxy + AutoDock Vina docking)
ROCS 3D shape alignment for ligand-based design
virtual screening comparison via full enumeration (~300M compounds, S-scale)
data_sources: Reaction templates: Hartenfeller et al. (58 reactions) + Button et al. (64 reactions)
Protein Data Bank (PDB) structures: AURKA 2BMC, DRD2 6CM4, sEH 4JNC
Therapeutic Data Commons (sEH QSAR model reference)
core_conclusion: SynGFN integrates synthesis planning into a GFlowNet-based generative model by treating molecular assembly as a cascade of simulated chemical reactions over synthesizable building blocks. Its hierarchically pretrained dual-agent policy explores a chemical space up to an order of magnitude larger than competing synthesis-aware models (up to 70x more #Circles coverage for top-1000 subsets) while guaranteeing synthesizability. In a wet-lab validation, 10 of 10 proposed GluN1/GluN3A inhibitor candidates were synthesized within 30 days and 6 showed measurable inhibitory activity, with the best compound achieving IC50 = 2.68 uM.
why_it_matters: Closes the synthesizability dilemma in AI drug discovery; achieves order-of-magnitude larger chemical space coverage vs. baselines; multifidelity acquisition reduces costly docking evaluations ~10x; wet-lab case doubles synthesis throughput; scales to ~100,000-reactant industrial libraries via hierarchical pretraining.
why_nature: Cross-disciplinary integration of generative deep learning, combinatorial chemistry, and Bayesian optimization with unusually strong wet-lab validation; introduces a reusable topology-inspired coverage metric (#Circles); addresses a field-wide bottleneck (synthesizability gap) with translational impact on pharmaceutical R&D.
added_value_to_my_research: GFlowNet MDP with dual-agent hierarchical policy -> blueprint for diversity-preserving spatial facility-location optimizer with service-type + location dual-action structure
Multifidelity acquisition (cheap proxy first, expensive oracle later) -> active learning for spatial data fusion using mobile-phone records as cheap proxy and GPS/survey data as expensive oracle
#Circles packing-number coverage metric -> novel spatial diversity metric for evaluating geographic coverage breadth of healthcare or EV-charging facility networks
GNN state representation as drop-in replacement for Morgan fingerprints -> physics-informed graph-learning architecture transferable to road-network accessibility estimation

Generative assembly of molecules from building blocks via reaction rules -> generative urban region design where land-use types are assembled under zoning constraints guided by multi-objective GFlowNet

related.title: Machine learning-aided generative molecular design
related.relation: broad review of ML-based generative models for drug discovery; provides context and baseline landscape for SynGFN

related.title: Traversing chemical space with active deep learning for low-data drug discovery
related.relation: active learning for chemical space exploration under data scarcity; complementary to SynGFN's multifidelity acquisition strategy

related.title: Rapid traversal of vast chemical space using machine learning-guided docking screens
related.relation: ML-guided docking to traverse large make-on-demand libraries; directly comparable to SynGFN's chemical space exploration efficiency

related.title: A deep learning approach for rational ligand generation with toxicity control via reactive building blocks
related.relation: building-block-based ligand generation with multi-property control; shares the synthesis-from-fragments paradigm with SynGFN

related.title: Learning characteristics of graph neural networks predicting protein-ligand affinities
related.relation: GNN-based protein-ligand affinity prediction; relevant to replacing Morgan fingerprint policy representations in SynGFN with graph-based encoders

related.title: Molecular deep learning at the edge of chemical space
related.relation: out-of-distribution generalization of molecular models at the edges of known chemical space; directly relevant to SynGFN's cross-distribution pretraining goals

figures.caption: Overview of the SynGFN algorithm: (a) hierarchical dual-agent MDP for reaction-based molecular assembly, (b) SynGFN's role bridging design and make in the AI-driven DMTA cycle, (c) example generated molecules with explicit synthetic routes for three drug targets.
